description: Add a color property to shopping lists with a 10-color swatch picker and full-color row backgrounds

userStories.title: Database Migration — Add color column to lists
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a color column on the lists table so that each list can store its associated color
acceptanceCriteria: Migration file exists at supabase/migrations/YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_add_list_color.sql
SQL is: ALTER TABLE lists ADD COLUMN color text NOT NULL DEFAULT '#1565c0'
Existing lists in the database have color = '#1565c0' after migration
New lists inserted without a color value default to '#1565c0'
No RLS policy errors — existing CRUD operations on lists still work
Lint passes
userStories.testIntensityReason: Single ALTER TABLE with default value, no logic

userStories.title: Service Layer & Context — Wire color through data flow
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the color field passed through create, update, subscribe, and optimistic update flows so that the UI can read and write list colors
acceptanceCriteria: createList in database.js accepts a color parameter and includes it in the INSERT
Omitting color in createList defaults to '#1565c0'
updateList in database.js maps color in the allowed update fields
subscribeLists returns list objects that include the color property
createListAction in ShoppingListContext.jsx accepts color, passes to dbCreateList, and includes it in the optimistic state update
updateListDetailsAction allows color in the updates object
Lint passes
userStories.testIntensityReason: Touches 3 functions across 2 files in the data flow

userStories.title: Color Picker in Create & Edit Forms
userStories.description: As a user, I want to choose a color when creating or editing a list so that I can visually distinguish my lists
acceptanceCriteria: LIST_PRESET_COLORS constant defined at top of ListSelector.jsx with 10 hex values: #1565c0, #2e7d32, #c62828, #ef6c00, #6a1b9a, #00838f, #ad1457, #f9a825, #37474f, #4e342e
Create form shows 10 color swatches below the name input, above the Create button
Blue (#1565c0) is pre-selected by default in the create form
Tapping a swatch selects it with visual feedback (border + scale, matching StoreManager pattern)
Selected color is passed through onCreate(name, emoji, color)
Edit form shows the same 10 swatches with the list's current color pre-selected
Changing color in edit form and saving persists the new color via onUpdateDetails(id, { name, emoji, color })
Menu option text reads 'Name, Icon & Color' on desktop dropdown (was 'Name & Icon')
Menu option text reads 'Name, Icon & Color' on mobile action sheet (was 'Name & Icon')
CSS classes .colorPicker, .colorSwatch, .colorSelected added to ListSelector.module.css
Swatches are usable on mobile (adequate touch targets, min 36px tap area with spacing)
Works in both light and dark mode
Lint passes
userStories.testIntensityReason: UI form changes with state management across create and edit flows

userStories.title: Display List Color as Full Row Background
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see my list's color as the row background so that I can instantly identify lists by color
acceptanceCriteria: Each list row has its full list color as the background — no opacity or alpha suffix (remove the current '1A' approach in getRowTintStyle)
All text on colored rows is white (#fff) and fully readable
Item count text (.listCount) is slightly dimmed white (rgba(255,255,255,0.7)) for visual hierarchy
Chevron is white on colored rows
Three-dot menu button is white and visible on all row colors including lighter ones (yellow #f9a825)
Shared badge is readable on colored backgrounds (white text on rgba(255,255,255,0.2) pill)
Active list row is visually distinguishable while keeping its color (use filter: brightness(1.15) or inset white left border, NOT --primary-tint background)
Hover state provides visual feedback without losing the color (use filter: brightness shift)
Row separators are visible against colored backgrounds (use rgba(255,255,255,0.15) border instead of var(--border-subtle))
Mobile rounded corners are preserved — colored backgrounds respect border-radius on first/last child
Works in both light and dark mode
Lint passes
userStories.notes: This replaces the previous low-opacity tint approach. The key change: remove getRowTintStyle's alpha suffix ('1A') and use full backgroundColor: list.color with white text. Active state should use brightness filter or white left border instead of --primary-tint. Also need to override .menuBtn color on hover:hover media query since it uses color: transparent by default.
userStories.testIntensityReason: Visual styling rework with multiple CSS overrides for text, borders, active/hover states across light and dark mode
